note: Beat reporter definitions for parallel agent show generation. 2026-06-13: added context_file per beat (1:1 map to .claude/context/beats/*.md, the on-demand editorial guidance loaded by /run-beat-reporters) and an economics_culture beat. 2026-06-11: AI block anchored on AI Daily Brief + Moonshots; added the claude_lab Build-Pitch Reporter; broadened sports to ramp NFL as NBA winds down.

beats.name: AI & Technology
beats.editorial_notes: FOCUS ON HIGH-SIGNAL AI (2026-06-11). The two anchor sources are the AI Daily Brief (Nathaniel Whittemore) and Moonshots (Peter Diamandis) — lead the AI block with their framing whenever a fresh episode exists, and give each real airtime, not a passing mention. Build around their actual arguments and quotes; use the RSS news feeds (Techmeme, TechCrunch, Ars) only as context/headlines around the podcast discussion. Then feature high-signal essays and technical breakthroughs getting discussion across rationalist/tech circles. Skip generic news summaries. Hand off agent-harness / how-to-build material to the Agents & Building beat and Claude-technique findings to the Build-Pitch Reporter (claude_lab).

beats.name: Agents & Building With AI
beats.editorial_notes: FEATURED BEAT. How people are actually running their agents: personalized harness structures, CLAUDE.md / context engineering, subagents and multi-agent orchestration, project organization, evals, MCP and tooling, dev-loop optimization. Frame every story through 'what can WE steal for our own multi-agent setup' — Kyle is building a team of delegated AIs and wants to optimize that system. Practitioner-level and specific; quote the actual techniques. INCLUDES the Build-Pitch of the Day: read .tmp/build-pitches.md (output of the claude_lab reporter) and, if a verified pitch exists, give it a dedicated sub-segment — what the technique is, who's doing it, the evidence it's real, and how it'd upgrade Kyle's stack — and say on-air that it's logged in build-pitches/ for Kyle to greenlight.

beats.name: Claude Lab — Build-Pitch Reporter
beats.editorial_notes: SYSTEM-OPTIMIZATION SCOUT (reframed 2026-06-18). This beat's lens is Kyle's WHOLE SYSTEM, not the podcast. Its job: answer, from a whole-fleet vantage, 'what is the single highest-leverage thing Kyle could build or adopt right now to improve his entire Agent OS?' — then verify it and surface it. The podcast is the delivery surface; build-pitches/ is also a feed the weekly Fleet Optimizer survey reads for its slate. LENS (do BEFORE scanning): (1) Read the fleet's current biggest needs first so the hunt is aimed — ~/fleet-optimizer/STATUS.md, ~/.observer/wiki/agent-os/credit-resilience-plan.md, ~/.observer/wiki/agent-os/, and recent breakage/alarm patterns. (2) Rank candidates by LEVERAGE toward Kyle's priorities in order: income-screenwriting (via protecting his attention), income-AI-businesses (the multi-agent-without-Kyle unlock), resilience of the trading systems; whole-fleet beats single-project polish; score = leverage x evidence / cost. (3) ADAPTIVE search: derive keyword queries from the needs you just read, not a fixed list — what you hunt for changes as the system changes. Scan recent (~5-7 days) YouTube transcripts from the claude_lab channels plus those aimed searches. VERIFY before reporting: cross-check against other sources, confirm real evidence (not a demo/hype), discard what's vague or what Kyle already runs. Do NOT treat the podcast as an optimization target — his stack is observer-system, the COS, the dispatcher/PR-reviewer loop, the multi-agent delegation setup, the trading sentinel/bots. You MAY surface a money/trading improvement but tag it 'money - discuss first' (not auto-buildable). Survivors become BUILD PITCHES with: technique, who's doing it (video+corroborating sources), evidence, the need it serves (which fleet pain + which priority), whole-fleet leverage (one line the Fleet Optimizer can rank on), and a build sketch. Write build-pitches/YYYY-MM-DD.md + a short .tmp/build-pitches.md for the episode writer. NEVER fabricate; if nothing clears the bar, say so.

beats.name: Sports — NFL (ramping) & NBA (winding down)
beats.editorial_notes: RAMP NFL UP AS NBA WINDS DOWN (2026-06-11). NFL football is now the growing half of this beat — lead with it. The Ringer Fantasy Football Show is Kyle's named anchor; also pull Fantasy Footballers and Bill Barnwell. Cover rankings, values/busts, roster strategy, real-football roster moves and league trends, and pull specific analyst quotes from the transcripts. NBA is winding down (Finals/offseason) — keep it to genuinely notable storylines (Finals, draft, major trades/free agency) and downweight volume, don't eliminate. As the calendar moves toward NFL training camp and the season, keep shifting weight from NBA to NFL.

beats.name: Entertainment & Film/TV
beats.editorial_notes: Industry news, deals, greenlights, box office. Kyle is a screenwriter/producer — engage at that level. Matthew Belloni and Puck are highest signal.

beats.name: Economics & Culture
beats.editorial_notes: Best pieces from the rationalist/policy/econ blogosphere. Substack essays are the richest material here (Noah Smith on economics, Scott Alexander, Zvi, Dean Ball on policy). When an article shows Type: substack (full text) in the brief, read the full article from .tmp/articles/ and integrate the key arguments and quotes so listeners get the takeaways without reading a 10,000-word post. Include only when there is something genuinely interesting; this is a back-half beat, not a guaranteed segment.

beats.name: Prediction Markets (quick-hit, de-emphasized)
beats.editorial_notes: DE-EMPHASIZED as of 2026-06-09 — Kyle's focus has moved off prediction markets. Include at most ONE short quick-hit, and only if there is genuinely notable movement (a big position change, a market resolving, a real edge). Otherwise skip entirely. No full trading block.

show_structure.intro: Cold open with the biggest story, then show name + date
show_structure.segments: 4-7 segments across beats, each with specific quotes from sources
show_structure.quick_hits: 2-3 shorter items that didn't warrant full segments
